cultures.title: Albania
cultures.description: Albania staged as a culture: a tongue kin to none, a sworn word, a nation above creed, scattered and rising.

cultures.title: Antigua and Barbuda
cultures.description: Two small islands that turned a sugar colony's chains into a free nation with a wide reach.

cultures.title: Argentina
cultures.description: A nation of immigrants and passion, haunted by a greatness it once held and lost.

cultures.title: Australia
cultures.description: A young nation on the oldest land, closing the gap between the fair go and its first peoples.

cultures.title: Austria
cultures.description: Austria staged as a culture: the same German tongue as its neighbour, in a softer, baroque, charming key.

cultures.title: The Bahamas
cultures.description: An archipelago of seven hundred islands, sharing the wealth of its sea and steering its own course.

cultures.title: Barbados
cultures.description: An island built on the plantation it pioneered, now a republic reckoning with its own creation.

cultures.title: Belarus
cultures.description: Belarus staged as a culture: a long-quiet nation whose chancery tongue, once an empire's, now wakes.

cultures.title: Belgium
cultures.description: Belgium staged as a culture: a country with no common tongue, held across its language border.

cultures.title: Belize
cultures.description: A small, late-born, English-speaking nation holding its many peoples as one between forest and reef.

cultures.title: Bolivia
cultures.description: Bolivia escenificada como cultura: la nación más indígena de América, una y plural en lo alto de los Andes.

cultures.title: Bosnia and Herzegovina
cultures.description: Bosnia staged as a culture: the bridge between East and West, three peoples and one tongue, bound and divided.

cultures.title: Brazil
cultures.description: A continental nation of three peoples, the country of the future still arriving.

cultures.title: Bulgaria
cultures.description: Bulgaria staged as a culture: the nation that gave the Slavs their alphabet and held through five centuries of yoke.

cultures.title: Canada
cultures.description: Canada staged as a culture: a bilingual federation of two peoples and a mosaic, reckoning with its founding wrong.

cultures.title: Chile
cultures.description: A long sober land of poets between the Andes and the sea, reckoning with its memory.

cultures.title: China
cultures.description: China staged as a culture: a civilization that has endured five thousand years, one writing binding the many.

cultures.title: Colombia
cultures.description: A land of magical realism and resilient joy, on the long road from its wound to peace.

cultures.title: Costa Rica
cultures.description: Costa Rica en escena: una pequeña democracia sin ejercito que guarda su paz, su igualdad y su verde abundancia.

cultures.title: Croatia
cultures.description: Croatia staged as a culture: the Adriatic bulwark of Christendom, late to its own state.

cultures.title: Cuba
cultures.description: Cuba en escena: una isla de música y resistencia, partida entre dos orillas.

cultures.title: Cyprus
cultures.description: Cyprus staged as a culture: an island crossroads, Greek and Turkish, split since 1974 and still unhealed.

cultures.title: Czechia
cultures.description: Czechia staged as a culture: a small, skeptical, godless nation that survives by irony, not by heroics.

cultures.title: Denmark
cultures.description: Denmark staged as a culture: a flat, trusting land where no one stands above and the good-enough wins.

cultures.title: Dominica
cultures.description: The Nature Isle, last refuge of the Kalinago, a wild Creole nation rebuilding stronger after the storm.

cultures.title: Dominican Republic
cultures.description: La cuna del Nuevo Mundo: memoria, una isla compartida y un pueblo de ritmo y resiliencia.

cultures.title: Ecuador
cultures.description: El Ecuador puesto en escena: un pequeño país plurinacional en la mitad del mundo, entre sierra y costa.

cultures.title: El Salvador
cultures.description: El Salvador en escena: el país más pequeño del istmo, herido por su guerra y unido a sus hijos lejanos.

cultures.title: England
cultures.description: England staged as a culture: a green, ironic land of fair play, the amateur, and mustn't grumble.

cultures.title: Estonia
cultures.description: Estonia staged as a culture: a small Finnic nation, long ruled by others, reborn through song and code.

cultures.title: Fiji
cultures.description: Na lewe rua e tiko e dua na vanua: na iTaukei kei na kai Idia, e dua na Viti.

cultures.title: Finland
cultures.description: Finland staged as a culture: a quiet, hardy land of forest and lake where sisu, equality, and trust hold it together.

cultures.title: France
cultures.description: France staged as a culture: a republic of ideas and grandeur, where the state, laicite and the art de vivre meet.

cultures.title: Germany
cultures.description: Germany staged as a culture: the correct way found by competence, said plainly, kept across a long memory.

cultures.title: Greece
cultures.description: Greece staged as a culture: the oldest living European tongue, carried three thousand years through every empire.

cultures.title: Grenada
cultures.description: The Spice Isle, healing the memory of its revolution and carrying a small nation forward as one people.

cultures.title: Guatemala
cultures.description: Guatemala como cultura: el corazón vivo del mundo maya y un país de muchos pueblos por reconciliar.

cultures.title: Guyana
cultures.description: One plural nation of many peoples and rivers, healing its divide and stewarding a sudden windfall.

cultures.title: Haiti
cultures.description: Premye repiblik nwa a, ki te libere tet li, kanpe ak fyete sou chimen diyite.

cultures.title: Honduras
cultures.description: Honduras puesta en escena: una nacion moldeada desde fuera que busca su soberania y su dignidad.

cultures.title: Hungary
cultures.description: Hungary staged as a culture: a people alone in Europe, bound by a kinless tongue, a wound, and doomed defiance.

cultures.title: Iceland
cultures.description: Iceland staged as a culture: a small, tough island nation that trusts it will sort itself out and lets none stand above.

cultures.title: Ireland
cultures.description: Ireland staged as a culture: a nation that reveres a first language it barely speaks and lives in English.

cultures.title: Italy
cultures.description: Italy staged as a culture: a civilization before a state, its national tongue laid late over a peninsula of dialects.

cultures.title: Jamaica
cultures.description: A small island that became a global cultural superpower, likkle but tallawah.

cultures.title: Kiribati
cultures.description: Te botanaomata n Mikronesia i aon abamwakoro aika a uarereke, e tei imwain te taari ae e a rake.

cultures.title: Latvia
cultures.description: Latvia staged as a culture: a peasant nation under foreign lords that sang itself into being and freedom.

cultures.title: Lithuania
cultures.description: Lithuania staged as a culture: an archaic Baltic nation, once vast, that survived a press ban and exile to break free.

cultures.title: Luxembourg
cultures.description: Luxembourg staged as a culture: a micro-state that lives in three languages and survives by remaining what it is.

cultures.title: Malta
cultures.description: Malta staged as a culture: a tiny island fortress at the heart of the Mediterranean, Semitic in tongue yet Catholic.

cultures.title: Marshall Islands
cultures.description: Aelon in ri-jerakrok eo, kinie baam eo, im juon aelon ej jutak iturin lometo eo ej wanlontak.

cultures.title: Mexico
cultures.description: Una nación mestiza nacida del choque de dos mundos, cerrando la herida de la conquista.

cultures.title: Micronesia
cultures.description: A federation of four island tongues held as one nation by English and a shared ocean.

cultures.title: Montenegro
cultures.description: Montenegro staged as a culture: a warrior nation of the Black Mountain whose tongue is split by its own name.

cultures.title: Nauru
cultures.description: Naoero: ribanan rik, eb me eo aen me imin boe, ngo etsi eko ma dorerin.

cultures.title: Netherlands
cultures.description: The Netherlands staged as a culture: a low country won from the sea by tolerance and consensus.

cultures.title: New Zealand
cultures.description: Two peoples bound by a founding treaty, an egalitarian nation at the edge of the world.

cultures.title: Nicaragua
cultures.description: Nicaragua, tierra de lagos y volcanes y de poetas, dos veces dividida entre el Pacifico y el Caribe.

cultures.title: North Macedonia
cultures.description: North Macedonia staged as a culture: a young nation whose name, tongue, church and history each neighbour contests.

cultures.title: Northern Ireland
cultures.description: Northern Ireland staged as a culture: two traditions held in one small place, and a hard-won peace they made together.

cultures.title: Norway
cultures.description: Norway staged as a culture: a steep land of mountains and sea where people belong to nature and share the work.

cultures.title: Palau
cultures.description: A Belau el beluu el oba a telungalek ma bai, e diak a nuclear, e mesisiich a omenged.

cultures.title: Panama
cultures.description: Panamá puesta como cultura: el istmo, el cruce del mundo, un crisol que pelea por su soberanía.

cultures.title: Papua New Guinea
cultures.description: Wanpela kantri wantaim 800 tok ples, holim wantaim long Tok Pisin na wantok.

cultures.title: Paraguay
cultures.description: Una nación bilingüe forjada en el aislamiento y la pérdida, que guarda su alma guaraní y se rehízo de pie.

cultures.title: Peru
cultures.description: Una nación de todas las sangres que busca volver compartida su antigua grandeza andina.

cultures.title: Poland
cultures.description: Poland staged as a culture: a nation that outlived its own erasure, held by tongue and faith.

cultures.title: Portugal
cultures.description: Portugal staged as a culture: a small Atlantic land of saudade, gentle ways, and a vast seafaring memory.

cultures.title: Romania
cultures.description: Romania staged as a culture: a Latin island in a Slavic-Hungarian sea, descended from Rome's Dacia.

cultures.title: Russia
cultures.description: Russia staged as a culture: a vast land and a strong state, a great soul and literature, and the weight they carry.

cultures.title: Saint Kitts and Nevis
cultures.description: The smallest nation of the Americas, two islands cradle of the sugar empire, holding its federation beyond the cane.

cultures.title: Saint Lucia
cultures.description: A small Creole island fought over fourteen times that gave the world two Nobel laureates.

cultures.title: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
cultures.description: An archipelago of deep Garifuna roots under a living volcano, scattered isles carried forward as one.

cultures.title: Samoa
cultures.description: Samoa o le fatu o Polenisia, o le faaSamoa ma le aiga e tausi i le lalolagi fou.

cultures.title: Schleswig-Holstein
cultures.description: Schleswig-Holstein staged as a culture: a land between the seas, steadied by few words and a high dyke.

cultures.title: Scotland
cultures.description: Scotland staged as a culture: a proud, egalitarian nation that kept its mind, its kirk, and its independence of spirit.

cultures.title: Serbia
cultures.description: Serbia staged as a culture: an Orthodox nation of defiant inat, marked by the Kosovo covenant and loss.

cultures.title: Slovakia
cultures.description: Slovakia staged as a culture: a young nation, long ruled by others, that found its voice and its own state late.

cultures.title: Slovenia
cultures.description: Slovenia staged as a culture: a small Alpine nation that left Yugoslavia almost bloodlessly and turned West.

cultures.title: Solomon Islands
cultures.description: Solomon Aelan: planti aelan, planti tok ples, wanfala wantok.

cultures.title: Spain
cultures.description: Spain staged as a culture: a plurinational land of warmth and family, one and many at once.

cultures.title: Suriname
cultures.description: Het meest plurale land van de Amerika's, een natie als een boom met vele bladeren.

cultures.title: Sweden
cultures.description: Sweden staged as a culture: a reserved land of consensus and lagom, where the people's home keeps everyone in.

cultures.title: Switzerland
cultures.description: Switzerland staged as a culture: a nation made by will, holding four languages together as one.

cultures.title: Taiwan
cultures.description: Taiwan staged as a culture: a layered island of many peoples, faiths, and tongues, made new by its own hands.

cultures.title: Tonga
cultures.description: Ko Tonga ko e fonua Pasifika na'e 'ikai 'aupito pule'i, mo'ui pe 'a e Tu'i mo e tui.

cultures.title: Trinidad and Tobago
cultures.description: A plural, oil-rich twin-island nation that turned its callaloo mix into pan, mas and letters.

cultures.title: Turkey
cultures.description: Turkey staged as a culture: a steppe people astride two continents, between a secular republic and a devout heartland.

cultures.title: Tuvalu
cultures.description: Te fenua foliki e tu i te tafa o te moana, e tau ma te peau e maluga aka.

cultures.title: Ukraine
cultures.description: Ukraine staged as a culture: a distinct nation defending its freedom, dignity, and survival against an empire.

cultures.title: United Kingdom
cultures.description: The United Kingdom staged as a culture: a union of nations bound by law, understatement, and fair play.

cultures.title: Uruguay
cultures.description: Una pequeña república laica que cuida su rara igualdad y pega por encima de su tamaño.

cultures.title: United States
cultures.description: A nation founded on a creed, forever closing the gap between its promise and its reality.

cultures.title: Vanuatu
cultures.description: Wan nasen blong kastom mo tufala kala blong waet man, hem i stap hapi long fored laen blong klaemet.

cultures.title: Venezuela
cultures.description: La cuna de la independencia de un continente, hoy entre el petróleo y la diáspora.

cultures.title: Wales
cultures.description: Wales staged as a culture: a small nation that lost its princes and kept its language, its song, and its solidarity.

groups.title: the Anglosphere
groups.description: The Anglosphere as a group: five English-speaking nations bound by language, law, and the Five Eyes.

groups.title: The Baltics
groups.description: The Baltics as a group: Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania, bound by a shared occupation and a sung freedom.

groups.title: Benelux
groups.description: Benelux as a group: Belgium, the Netherlands, and Luxembourg, the small delta union that prototyped Europe.

groups.title: the Caribbean Community
groups.description: CARICOM as a group: the Caribbean Community, small states bound by a plantation past and a shared future.

groups.title: Central America
groups.description: Central America as a group: the isthmus of the SICA bloc, from the Maya world to a system of integration.

groups.title: DACH
groups.description: The German-speaking sphere as a group: Germany, Austria, and Switzerland, bound by a common tongue, not a common state.

groups.title: the European Union
groups.description: The European Union as a group: twenty-seven nations that pooled their sovereignty for peace and prosperity.

groups.title: the Hispanophone World
groups.description: Hispanidad as a group: the Spanish-speaking world, from Spain across the Americas, one tongue over an ocean.

groups.title: Iberia
groups.description: Iberia as a group: Spain and Portugal, the peninsula that once divided the world between its two crowns.

groups.title: Latin America
groups.description: Latin America as a group: the Iberian-and-French Americas bound by Latin tongues, faith, and mestizaje.

groups.title: the Lusophone World
groups.description: The Lusophone world as a group: Portugal and Brazil, one tongue carried across the Atlantic by the sea.

groups.title: the Southern Common Market
groups.description: Mercosur as a group: the Southern Cone common market, born of rivals reconciled into a river-basin union.

groups.title: NATO
groups.description: NATO as a group: the transatlantic alliance that grew from twelve to thirty-two, bound by Article 5.

groups.title: The Nordics
groups.description: The Nordics as a group: Denmark, Sweden, Norway, Finland, and Iceland, bound by a shared model and a common north.

groups.title: the Americas
groups.description: The Americas as a group: two continents and the Caribbean, the New World from first peoples to republics.

groups.title: the Andean Community
groups.description: The Andes as a group: the Andean Community, heirs of the Inca along the great cordillera.

groups.title: The Visegrad Four
groups.description: The Visegrad Four as a group: Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and Hungary, bound by the return to Europe.
